macro_context.headline: US-Iran peace-deal reports collapse oil to 94, lift equities to fresh records — AMD's AI beat and Anthropic's xAI Colossus capacity deal supercharge the tech rally.

EURUSD.name: Euro / US Dollar
EURUSD.past: EUR/USD pushed back above 1.1750 (+0.49%) as the dollar sold off broadly on US-Iran peace-deal headlines that tanked oil and revived risk appetite.
EURUSD.why: Lower oil reads as a positive Eurozone terms-of-trade shock and removes the stagflation tail that capped the euro at 1.17. The ECB-hawk / Fed-soft trade is back on the table.
EURUSD.watch: Hold above 1.1740 keeps the door open to retest 1.1800 overnight; failure there snaps the move and exposes 1.1700. Friday US NFP is the bigger test.

headlines.title: Sterling Today: Pound jumps as dollar sells off on Iran-Hormuz deal hopes
Oil Shock + Hawkish Fed = Major EUR/USD Downside? Don't Miss These Trading Opportunities
Eurozone inflation hits 3% as oil prices spike and economic growth slows

GBPUSD.name: British Pound / US Dollar
GBPUSD.past: Cable pinned near 1.3593 — broadly unchanged but with a sharp intraday spike on Iran-deal headlines before drifting back as UK election risk capped follow-through.
GBPUSD.why: Sterling is caught between two opposing forces today: dollar weakness lifts it, but Bloomberg's UK election warning is keeping fast money cautious from extending longs.
GBPUSD.watch: 1.3625 is the breakout pivot — clean break opens 1.3700; failure to hold 1.3550 reopens 1.3500 with election risk as the catalyst.

headlines.title: Yen Spikes to 10-Week High and Sparks Intervention Chatter
UK Elections Threaten to End the Pound’s Resilient Run
Sterling Today: Pound jumps as dollar sells off on Iran-Hormuz deal hopes

USDJPY.name: US Dollar / Japanese Yen
USDJPY.past: USD/JPY dropped to 156.37 as the dollar weakened broadly. Yen got a relief bid even with US10Y still elevated near 4.42%.
USDJPY.why: The pair is decoupling from yields short-term — risk-on usually hurts yen, but Iran de-escalation lifted JPY on safe-haven unwind from oil-importer angle.
USDJPY.watch: Tokyo CPI Friday is the next yen-mover. Below 154 opens 152; back above 158 means oil-shock fears returning and Iran optimism fading.

headlines.title: Why a Plunging Dollar Is Missing Out on the Relief Rally
How Markets Reacted to Trump's Iran Post, in Charts
The Dollar Isn’t Done. Why It’s Outshining Rivals Amid Iran Crisis.

AUDUSD.name: Australian Dollar / US Dollar
AUDUSD.past: AUD/USD ripped to 0.7250 (+1.14%) — Asian risk rally plus weak dollar plus Samsung's $1T milestone fed the Aussie bid through 0.7240.
AUDUSD.why: AUD is the cleanest expression of the Iran-truce + AI-rally trade. China stimulus chatter is icing, but the move is global risk-on, not local fundamentals.
AUDUSD.watch: RBA minutes due; if any hawkish dissent surfaces, 0.7300 prints. China PMI later in the week is the bigger test — soft print kills this rally fast.

headlines.title: Dollar softens as Middle East ceasefire holds
Why is the Australian Dollar Rallying?
The Next Trade You Should Make Comes from the Land Down Under

XAUUSD.name: Gold
XAUUSD.past: Gold rallied to 4,697 (+0.56%) as the dollar weakened on Iran peace-deal reports — the safe-haven bid stayed intact even as risk assets rallied.
XAUUSD.why: Today's tape says gold is now a dollar-debasement / real-yield play, not a war hedge. The move with weaker DXY plus stocks at records is the unusual signal — gold is decoupling from fear.
XAUUSD.watch: 4,720 is the next resistance; clean break opens 4,800 into Friday NFP. Failure to hold 4,650 risks a quick 4,580 retest if Iran-deal optimism deepens.

headlines.title: Gold, silver jump on report that the US and Iran are nearing a peace deal
Amex Gold vs. Chase Sapphire Preferred: Two of the best travel cards for everyday rewards
Update: Gold Moves Higher as the Dollar and Yields Retreat Following Reports the Iran War May Be Near an End

XAGUSD.name: Silver
XAGUSD.past: Silver popped to 77.74 (+1.99%), outperforming gold by ~3-to-1 on the Iran-deal-driven dollar slide and continued industrial-demand bid from datacenter / solar build-out.
XAGUSD.why: Silver's beta to a softer dollar plus its industrial leverage to the AI-infrastructure boom is the cleanest expression of today's risk-on plus dollar-down combo.
XAGUSD.watch: Above 78 puts 80 in play — that's the level that triggered the AGQ backwardation chatter last week. Below 76 and the rally fails.

headlines.title: Gold, silver jump on report that the US and Iran are nearing a peace deal
Gold and silver prices today, Wednesday, May 6: Gold and silver prices up significantly this morning
Eaton Datacenter Orders Surge, Growth Outlook Strengthens Into H2, RBC Says

USOIL.name: WTI Crude Oil
USOIL.past: WTI crashed to 94.53 (-6.5%, -$6.55) as reports surfaced that the US and Iran are nearing a peace deal — the war-premium that had oil at $101 unwound in hours.
USOIL.why: This is the cleanest macro pivot of the year: every cross-asset move today (dollar down, stocks up, gold up) traces back to oil's collapse. The Iran headline is the single trade.
USOIL.watch: 92 is the next major support — break opens 88. If a formal deal text leaks tonight, gap risk to 90. If headlines are walked back, snap rally to 100 is in play.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Nasdaq, S&P 500, Dow rally on rising hopes for Iran peace, upbeat tech earnings
US gas prices rise above $4.50 as reports point to US-Iran peace deal
Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq notch fresh records as tech leads markets higher, oil falls

US500.name: S&P 500
US500.past: S&P 500 ripped to 7,354 (+1.31%, +95pts) — a fresh record close as oil's collapse and Iran-deal hopes ignited a broad-based risk-on bid led by tech and consumer discretionary.
US500.why: The Iran-deal narrative removes the single biggest macro overhang. With AMD's Q1 beat reinforcing the AI capex story, there's no offsetting bear case left for tape-chasers today.
US500.watch: 7,400 is the next round-number magnet for momentum; failure to hold 7,300 on a peace-deal walk-back opens 7,200. Friday NFP is the real test of breadth.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Nasdaq, S&P 500, Dow rally on rising hopes for Iran peace, upbeat tech earnings
AI infrastructure: Is it time to trade in the Mag 7 for the Air 7?
Equity Markets Rise Intraday, Oil Plunges Amid US-Iran Peace Deal Optimism

NAS100.name: Nasdaq 100
NAS100.past: Nasdaq 100 surged to 28,512 (+1.77%, +497pts) — fresh all-time high as NVDA, AMZN, GOOGL all rallied 2-3% on AMD's earnings halo and the Anthropic-Colossus AI capacity deal.
NAS100.why: Today is the AI-capex bull case in pure form: AMD beat says spending is real, Anthropic taking all of Colossus says hyperscaler demand is uncapped, and oil's collapse de-risks margins.
NAS100.watch: 28,750 is the next technical level. Below 28,200 calls the breakout into question. NVDA earnings later this month is the next major catalyst.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Nasdaq, S&P 500, Dow rally on rising hopes for Iran peace, upbeat tech earnings
SpaceX isn't public yet, but Wall Street is already building ETFs for it
Stocks rise, oil sinks on hopes of U.S.-Iran deal

US30.name: Dow Jones
US30.past: Dow added 634 points to 49,933 (+1.29%) — laggard relative to tech but still printed fresh records as energy stocks held firm despite oil's collapse and rate-cut hopes lifted financials.
US30.why: The Dow's cyclical and value tilt is benefiting from the dual tailwind: lower oil cuts input costs for industrials, while the soft-dollar move helps multinational earnings translation.
US30.watch: 50,000 is the obvious psychological line — clean break is a momentum trigger; failure to hold 49,500 calls the rally into question.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Nasdaq, S&P 500, Dow rally on rising hopes for Iran peace, upbeat tech earnings
Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq notch fresh records as tech leads markets higher, oil falls
Equity Markets Rise Intraday, Oil Plunges Amid US-Iran Peace Deal Optimism

BTC.name: Bitcoin
BTC.past: Bitcoin held its 80,927 level — flat on the session, consolidating after Tuesday's push past 81,000 as risk-on flows favored equities over crypto today.
BTC.why: The 'bitcoin as macro hedge' narrative is being tested: with oil collapsing and stocks at records, BTC is acting like a high-beta tech proxy, not a safe haven, and seeing modest profit-taking.
BTC.watch: 82,000 is resistance; 79,000 is the line that turns the trend negative. Strategy's potential rebalance flagged in today's headlines is the wildcard.

headlines.title: Gold, silver jump on report that the US and Iran are nearing a peace deal
Bitcoin and ethereum prices today, Wednesday, May 6, 2026: Prices up; bitcoin at highest level since January
Strategy's $67 Billion Bitcoin Stance Starts To Shift

ETH.name: Ethereum
ETH.past: Ethereum flat at 2,361 — outflows from spot ETH ETFs continued per BeInCrypto, weighing on the asset even as BTC held firm.
ETH.why: ETH is suffering a relative rotation: the Iran-deal trade rewarded equities and oil-shorts directly, and ETH's lack of a clean narrative leaves it as a residual position to trim.
ETH.watch: 2,400 is resistance; below 2,300 risks a flush to 2,250. Coinbase earnings (after the close tomorrow) is the next cycle catalyst.

headlines.title: Bitcoin and ethereum prices today, Wednesday, May 6, 2026: Prices up; bitcoin at highest level since January
Mysterious trader files lawsuit against Coinbase ahead of earnings
Kevin O’Leary Warns Most Crypto Tokens Are Over, Says Enterprise Blockchain Is Crypto’s Next Phase

NVDA.name: Nvidia
NVDA.past: Nvidia ripped to 206.66 (+5.17%, +$10.16) — the standout single-stock move today, driven by AMD's Q1 AI-revenue beat that re-ratified the entire AI-capex thesis.
NVDA.why: AMD beat means the AI build-out is broader and deeper than feared; Anthropic taking all SpaceX Colossus capacity confirms the next tier of compute demand. NVDA is the purest expression.
NVDA.watch: 210 is the round-number target; 216.83 is the 52-week high. NVDA earnings later this month is the catalyst of the cycle. Below 200 and the breakout has failed.

headlines.title: Anthropic to rent all AI capacity at SpaceX's Colossus data center
The space ETFs prepping for liftoff ahead of SpaceX IPO
AMD stock surges on Q1 earnings, driven by AI demand boom

TSLA.name: Tesla
TSLA.past: Tesla advanced to 399.78 (+2.67%, +$10.41) on the broader risk rally and SpaceX Terafab chip-factory headlines reinforcing the Musk-conglomerate AI narrative.
TSLA.why: Tesla today is trading more on Musk-empire optionality (SpaceX, Terafab, robotaxi) than on Q-by-Q car deliveries. The $55B–$119B Terafab spend numbers reset the long-term AI-vertical story.
TSLA.watch: 400 is the obvious level to clear and hold; failure flips momentum and 380 comes back into play. Robotaxi rollout updates are the next stock-specific catalyst.

headlines.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX files initial $55 billion spend for Terafab chip factory in Texas
Tesla's 'fundamental approach' to robotaxis gives it an edge over Waymo
SpaceX may spend up to $119 billion on ‘Terafab’ chip factory in Texas

AAPL.name: Apple
AAPL.past: Apple climbed to 287.44 (+1.15%, +$3.26) — quiet outperformer on the broad rally, helped by reports of Apple exploring Intel/Samsung for chip fabrication diversification.
AAPL.why: AAPL is benefiting from two converging stories: the soft-dollar lifts foreign earnings translation, and the chip-supply diversification narrative reduces concentration risk in the multiple.
AAPL.watch: 290 is the clean level to break; 280 is the near-term floor. The CBS settlement headlines are noise — focus is on iPhone 18 launch cycle starting in September.

headlines.title: The space ETFs prepping for liftoff ahead of SpaceX IPO

iPhone owners could get up to $95 from a new Apple settlement

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.past: Microsoft added $2.34 to 413.72 (+0.57%) — quieter participant in today's tech rally, with the AI-capex narrative more directly benefiting NVDA/AMD/AMZN than the hyperscaler buyer.
MSFT.why: MSFT is the picks-and-shovels payer in the AI-capex story; today's tape rewards the picks-and-shovels sellers more directly. Anthropic's Colossus deal complicates the OpenAI-exclusive halo.
MSFT.watch: 420 is the round-number magnet; below 408 and the consolidation pattern breaks. Azure growth print at next earnings is the single number that moves the stock.

headlines.title: Anthropic to rent all AI capacity at SpaceX's Colossus data center

META.name: Meta Platforms
META.past: Meta climbed to 613.79 (+1.46%, +$8.83) — solid outperform on the AI-capex tape with Corning's Nvidia-deal headlines reinforcing the optical-connectivity build-out Meta is funding.
META.why: Meta's AI-capex program ($200B-class spend through 2026) suddenly looks rationally priced when Anthropic is renting an entire xAI datacenter — the demand side of Meta's spend is being re-rated.
META.watch: 620 is the breakout level; 600 is the line that holds the trend. Reels/AI ad-revenue commentary at the next earnings is the next stock-specific catalyst.

headlines.title: Micron stock is surging, hitting a new intraday record high. Why?

GOOGL.name: Alphabet
GOOGL.past: Alphabet rallied to 396.04 (+1.96%, +$7.61) on the Anthropic-Colossus headlines (Google is a major Anthropic backer) and the broader AI-rally lift to TIME100 mention.
GOOGL.why: Google's AI-infrastructure leverage is being re-rated: Anthropic's compute scaling reflects on Google Cloud demand, while Gemini-powered enterprise wins keep widening the moat narrative.
GOOGL.watch: 400 is the round-number target with TIME100 flow potentially adding pressure; 388 is the near-term floor. DOJ remedy ruling timeline is the wildcard hanging over the stock.

headlines.title: Anthropic to rent all AI capacity at SpaceX's Colossus data center

AMZN.name: Amazon
AMZN.past: Amazon added $3.22 to 276.77 (+1.18%) — solid participant with Andy Jassy's $200B AI-capex commitment headlines reinforcing the AWS narrative.
AMZN.why: AMZN's positioning is the cleanest blend of today's two themes: AWS captures AI-capex demand directly, while same-day grocery expansion and lower oil help the retail-margin story.
AMZN.watch: 280 is the breakout level — clean break opens 290; below 273 and the move is failing. AWS growth at next earnings is the single most important number.
